title: Surfing the Web on Internet Day
story: Every October 29, people around the world celebrate Internet Day by sharing their favorite websites, posting fun memes, and thanking the internet for connecting us all! Some schools teach kids about online safety and digital citizenship on this special day. Tech companies often share cool facts about how the internet works, while families might have fun exploring new educational websites together. Many people post throwback photos of old computers or share stories about their first time going online. It's a perfect day to appreciate how the internet lets us video chat with grandparents, play games with friends far away, and learn about anything we're curious about!
activity_question: What do many people post on Internet Day to remember the early days of computers?
follow_up_question: What's the coolest thing you've ever learned or discovered online?

title: Oats About Time for National Oatmeal Day!
story: Every bowl of oatmeal gets its moment to shine on October 29 during National Oatmeal Day! People celebrate by making their favorite oatmeal recipes, from classic brown sugar and cinnamon to wild toppings like chocolate chips, berries, or even savory ingredients. Social media fills with colorful oatmeal bowl photos showing off creative combinations. Some families have oatmeal cook-offs to see who makes the tastiest bowl!
activity_question: What do people often share on social media during National Oatmeal Day?
activity_choices: Photos of their colorful oatmeal bowls
activity_answer: Photos of their colorful oatmeal bowls
follow_up_question: What's the weirdest oatmeal topping combination you would try?

title: Hermit Day: Time to Hide Away!
story: October 29 is Hermit Day, when people celebrate the joy of staying home alone and doing absolutely nothing social! On this quirky holiday, hermits everywhere embrace their love of solitude by turning off their phones, avoiding crowds, and enjoying peaceful activities like reading, crafting, or binge-watching their favorite shows. Some people declare themselves "hermits for a day" and refuse to leave their houses, while others use it as an excuse to skip parties and social events guilt-free. It's the perfect day for introverts to recharge their batteries and enjoy some quality me-time without feeling weird about it!
activity_question: What do people typically do to celebrate Hermit Day?
activity_choices: Throw big parties with lots of friends
Stay home alone and enjoy solitude
activity_answer: Stay home alone and enjoy solitude
follow_up_question: What's your favorite way to spend quiet time alone at home?

title: Turkey's Big Birthday Bash
story: Every October 29, Turkey throws a massive birthday party for their entire country! Turkey Republic Day celebrates when Turkey became a republic and got its independence. People fill the streets waving red flags with white crescents and stars. Schools put on special programs where kids sing patriotic songs and perform traditional dances. Families gather for big feasts with delicious Turkish food like kebabs and baklava. Fireworks light up the sky in cities across the nation!
activity_question: What do people wave during Turkey Republic Day celebrations?
activity_choices: Red flags with white crescents and stars
activity_answer: Red flags with white crescents and stars
bonus_fact: Turkey Republic Day is also the anniversary of when Mustafa Kemal Atatürk became Turkey's first president!
